description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the node:8 Docker image and runs a before_script consisting of apt-get update, apt-get install -y sshpass, git config --global url.\"https://\".insteadOf git://, export PATH=$PATH:node_modules/.bin, npm install --unsafe-perm, ls -la, and ls node_modules/ before each job. Define variables GIT_SSL_NO_VERIFY set to true and SSHPASS taken from $SERVER_PASSWORD, and cache the node_modules/ directory per branch using the key $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME. Add a build job in the build stage that executes ng build and publishes the dist directory as an artifact that expires in one week. Add a test job in the test stage that echoes \"RUNNING TESTS\", runs ng test --code-coverage --single-run, and stores the coverage/* files as a one‑week artifact. Add three deploy jobs—development, production, and staging—each depending on the build job, setting the environment to development, production, or staging respectively; the development job runs only on the master branch and executes echo \"DEPLOY DEVELOPMENT\", echo \"$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY\", ls -la, ls node_modules/, and sshpass -e scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -r dist $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_HOST:/opt/personal-dashboard/current/, while the production and staging jobs run only on the production and staging branches and simply echo \"DEPLOY PRODUCTION\" or \"DEPLOY STAGING\".
